QF.id: XIII Introduction to the Underlying Markets_12

question.text: When did exchange-traded derivatives trading begin in India, and with which product?

answer.text: June 2000, with index futures on NIFTY and SENSEX
January 1999, with stock options on individual equities
March 2001, with currency futures on USDINR
November 2001, with commodity futures on gold

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI permitted BSE and NSE to introduce equity derivatives in June 2000, starting with index futures on NIFTY and SENSEX. Index options followed in June 2001, stock options in July 2001, and stock futures in November 2001. **The Trap:** Currency futures on exchanges started much later (2008). Commodity derivatives were also introduced later. The sequence of products is frequently tested. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch1 covers this history. Series I covers currency derivative introduction (2008). Series XVI covers commodity derivative history. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The chronological order of derivative introduction in India: Index Futures (Jun 2000) → Index Options (Jun 2001) → Stock Options (Jul 2001) → Stock Futures (Nov 2001).
